document-processing-services.h2: Intelligent Document Processing: How Automation Makes It Faster and More Accurate
document-processing-services.description: Traditional document processing relied on either manual entry (slow, error-prone) or basic OCR (fast, but limited to simple formats). Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) combines OCR, natural language processing, and machine learning to handle complex, variable, and unstructured documents that older approaches cannot manage reliably. Computyne uses IDP technology as part of a human-in-the-loop model: automated extraction handles the volume and speed, trained human validators catch the edge cases and ambiguous records that automation alone gets wrong. The result is faster processing than purely manual workflows and higher accuracy than fully automated approaches deliver on their own.

rows.description: Converts scanned images and PDFs into machine-readable text
Extends OCR to handle handwritten text and variable fonts
Identifies and extracts specific data fields from unstructured text
Improves extraction accuracy over time by learning from validated outputs
Expert reviewers catch edge cases that automated systems flag or miss
